## [39:25 - 42:26]

And you know, it's very macho response, but as copywriter, we can learn from that. I can't tell you how many times I've had a successful mailing or ad because I just didn't take what the client gave me. I said, well, how can we improve this? How can we, what you have is a little bit dry, a little bit academic if it's an informational product. I'm not gonna just accept this. How can we enhance this product or this offer to take what we want? If I had a magic wand and could create a product or an offer with almost no limits, let's do a little bit of that thinking at first. To see is there any way we can make it stronger? The worst mistake you can make as a copyright if you're dissatisfied is say, okay, well, I guess I gotta make the best of this hash. And then you work on it. Don't do that. Just see, let's imagine that you can take what you want, what would you want to make it better? Because in almost any one of the very first principles I learned from my first copy chief was that people just as easily buy the premium as they do the main product. And that gives you tremendous new flexibility now to sell something. Because you can almost always create a premium that is a red hot premium, even if the main product isn't so great. So in effect, you reverse the psychology where you think of, I'm gonna sell my product and the premium is an afterthought. Seems to work with people that you can emphasize the premium and they'll buy your product just to get that premium and the almost a product becomes an afterthought. Even if the product's halfway decent, they'll renew or be happy with it. So, if you take Al Davis's, much a remark, we don't take what we're given, we take what we want. Well, if you're a marketer then, bear that in mind when you wanna go out and whatever your product is, make it much more powerful. You can surround it with a great premium or just enhance it with a great premium. So your premium can be your most important secret weapon. For example, I had a newsletter once that was kind of dry. It was about international investing from a newsletter in Switzerland. And I really couldn't come up with anything. They're really that exciting. So we put our heads together and said, look, let's take what we want. What would investors really take notice of? He said, well, everybody's always intrigued with Swiss banks and how you can have secret bank accounts. And so the client came up with an offer that said, yours free, an ingot of pure gold bullion deposited in a secret number at Swiss bank account. I would take my newsletter and I'm gonna get you started. I'll open the account for you. I'm over here in Switzerland. I have relationships with old bankers. Don't send me any money except for the newsletter itself. I will open and get you your own secret number at Swiss bank account and I'll put your first little drop of gold bullion in there for you. Well, this thing just went through the roof because it was so much stronger than a report or insights from an international investor. People wanted to get that secret bank account opened. So and this is also the magic wand technique as I mentioned before. Just imagine you have a magic wand.
